info.title: Manta API
info.description: REST API for managing CSM/OpenCHAMI HPC clusters via manta.

get.summary:
GET /analysis/configurations — one row per CFS configuration, sorted by
`last_updated` ascending, with a `safe_to_delete` verdict derived
from cross-resource dependencies (CFS components' `desired_config`
and BSS-referenced images' built-with configuration). See
[`ConfigurationAnalysis`] for column semantics.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary:
GET /analysis/images — image-centric flat projection of every CFS
configuration, CFS session, BOS session template, and IMS image
visible to the caller. One row per IMS image; see
[`BackendSummary`] for column semantics.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: POST /api/v1/auth/token — exchange username/password for a CSM token.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: POST /api/v1/auth/validate — check whether a CSM token is still valid.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/boot-config` — apply BSS boot configuration to a set of nodes.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: GET /boot-parameters — fetch BSS boot parameters for a group or node list.

parameters.name: hsm_group
parameters.description: HSM group whose members' boot parameters should be returned.

parameters.name: nodes
parameters.description:
Explicit comma-separated xnames; mutually exclusive with
`hsm_group`.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

put.summary: PUT /boot-parameters — update boot image, kernel params, or runtime config for nodes.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: POST /boot-parameters — create a new BSS boot parameters entry.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

delete.summary: DELETE /boot-parameters — remove BSS boot parameter entries for specified hosts.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary:
DEPRECATED alias for `GET /groups/nodes`. Logs a server-side warning,
then delegates to the canonical handler. Old path kept for one
release.

parameters.name: hsm_group
parameters.description:
HSM group name to list nodes for. When omitted the response
covers every group the bearer token can access.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Optional power-status filter (e.g. `ON`, `OFF`, `READY`).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: GET /configurations — list CFS configurations with optional name/pattern/group filters.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Exact configuration name to fetch.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Glob pattern matched against configuration names.

parameters.name: hsm_group
parameters.description: HSM group whose associated configurations should be returned.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Cap on the number of configurations returned (most recent first).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/v1/configurations` — delete CFS configurations and all derived artifacts.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Glob pattern to match configuration names.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
ISO-8601 lower bound — only delete configurations created after
this date.

parameters.name: until
parameters.description:
ISO-8601 upper bound — only delete configurations created before
this date.

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.description: When true, return deletion candidates without removing anything.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/ephemeral-env` — launch an ephemeral CFS environment from an IMS image.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: GET /groups — list HSM groups, optionally filtered by name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Exact group name; returns all groups when `None`.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: POST /groups — create a new HSM group.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: GET /groups/available — list HSM group names the token can access.
get.description:
Backs CLI authorization helpers that used to call
`backend.get_group_name_available` directly.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: GET /groups/hardware — summarize hardware components per node for a group.

parameters.name: hsm_group
parameters.description:
HSM group name to inventory. When omitted the response covers
every group the bearer token can access.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: GET /groups/nodes — list nodes in a group with optional status filter.

parameters.name: hsm_group
parameters.description:
HSM group name to list nodes for. When omitted the response
covers every group the bearer token can access.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Optional power-status filter (e.g. `ON`, `OFF`, `READY`).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Group label

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Delete even if the group still has members (default: `false`).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: POST /groups/{name}/members — replace a group's member list from a host expression.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Group name

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/v1/groups/{name}/members` — remove nodes from an HSM group.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Group name

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary:
DEPRECATED alias for `GET /groups/hardware`. Logs a server-side
warning and delegates to the canonical handler. Old path kept for
one release.

parameters.name: hsm_group
parameters.description:
HSM group name to inventory. When omitted the response covers
every group the bearer token can access.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

200.description: [DEPRECATED] use /groups/hardware — hardware summary for group nodes

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/hardware-clusters/{target}/configuration` — pin or unpin nodes between clusters by hardware pattern.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Target cluster name

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/hardware-clusters/{target}/members` — move nodes matching a hardware pattern into a cluster.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site

get.summary: GET /hardware-nodes-list — hardware details for an explicit list of xnames.

parameters.name: xnames
parameters.description:
Hosts expression (xnames, NIDs, or hostlist notation). The field
name is retained for wire stability.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: GET /health — liveness probe; returns `{"status":"ok"}`.
GET /images — list IMS images sorted by creation time.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Exact IMS image ID; returns just that image when set.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description:
Glob pattern matched against image name; applied server-side
(`service::image::get_images`). Invalid glob returns 400.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Cap on the number of images returned (most recent first).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/v1/images` — delete IMS images by ID; validates only when `dry_run=true`.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of IMS image IDs to delete.

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.description:
When true, validate deletion eligibility without removing
anything.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

parameters.name: nodes

Explicit comma-separated xnames; mutually exclusive with

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/kernel-parameters/apply` — add, replace, or delete kernel parameters on nodes.

parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site

get.summary: GET /nodes — fetch node details for a given xname expression.

parameters.name: xname
parameters.description:
Comma-separated xnames, NIDs, or hostlist expression
(e.g. `x3000c0s1b0n[0-3]`).

parameters.name: include_siblings
parameters.description: Expand results to include nodes sharing the same blade.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Optional power-status filter (e.g. `ON`, `OFF`, `READY`).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: POST /nodes — register a new node in HSM and add it to a group.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

delete.summary: DELETE /nodes/{id} — remove a node from HSM by xname or NID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Node xname or NID

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: `WS /api/v1/nodes/{xname}/console` — attach an interactive PTY console to a node via WebSocket.

parameters.name: xname
parameters.description: Node xname

parameters.name: cols
parameters.description: Initial terminal width in columns (default `80`).

parameters.name: rows
parameters.description: Initial terminal height in rows (default `24`).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/power` — start a PCS power transition (on / off /
reset) against nodes or all members of a cluster and return the
transition id **immediately**. Does not block until the
transition completes — the CLI is responsible for polling
`GET /power/transitions/{id}` until the snapshot reports
`transitionStatus = "completed"`.
post.description:
Returns a `TransitionStartOutput` (`{ transitionID, operation }`)
as JSON. Callers can hand the `transitionID` to
[`get_power_transition`].

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/power/transitions/{id}` — fetch the current snapshot
of a PCS power transition (status, task counts, per-task detail).
Called by the CLI's poll loop after `POST /power` returns the id.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: GET /redfish-endpoints — list HSM Redfish endpoints with optional filters.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Exact endpoint ID (BMC xname) filter.

parameters.name: fqdn
parameters.description: FQDN substring filter.

parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: UUID exact-match filter.

parameters.name: macaddr
parameters.description: MAC-address exact-match filter (colon-separated hex).

parameters.name: ipaddress
parameters.description: IP-address exact-match filter (IPv4 or IPv6).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

put.summary: PUT /redfish-endpoints — update an existing Redfish endpoint's properties.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: POST /redfish-endpoints — register a new Redfish endpoint in HSM.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

delete.summary: DELETE /redfish-endpoints/{id} — remove a Redfish endpoint from HSM.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: BMC xname

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/sat-file/configurations` — apply a single SAT
configuration entry. Returns the created `CfsConfigurationResponse`.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/sat-file/images/cfs-session` — translate one SAT
`images[]` entry into a CFS session payload and create it. Returns
the freshly-created [`CfsSessionGetResponse`] so the CLI can drive
the monitor + stamp steps itself.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/sat-file/images/stamp` — fetch the named CFS session,
derive the provenance stamp, and PATCH the produced IMS image.
post.description:
Performs two boundary checks before delegating to the backend:
the caller must have access to every HSM group the session
targets, and the session must have produced a result image. See
[`crate::service::session::validate_session_access`] +
[`crate::service::session::require_result_image`].

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/sat-file/session-templates` — apply a single SAT
session_template entry. Returns the created BOS session template
and (if `reboot` was set and we're not in dry-run) the BOS session
that was kicked off to reboot the targeted nodes.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: GET /sessions — list CFS sessions with optional filters.

parameters.name: hsm_group
parameters.description: HSM group whose sessions should be returned.

parameters.name: xnames
parameters.description:
Filter to sessions whose `ansible_limit` mentions any of these
comma-separated xnames.

parameters.name: min_age
parameters.description:
Lower-bound session age expressed as a duration string
(e.g. `"1h"`, `"2d"`).

parameters.name: max_age
parameters.description: Upper-bound session age expressed as a duration string.

parameters.name: session_type
parameters.description: Session type filter: `"image"` or `"runtime"`.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Status filter: `"pending"`, `"running"`, or `"complete"`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Exact session name.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Cap on the number of sessions returned (most recent first).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/sessions` — create a CFS session from one or more git repositories.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

delete.summary: DELETE /sessions/{name} — cancel and delete a CFS session; `?dry_run=true` previews.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Session name

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.description:
When true, return deletion context without actually deleting
(default: `false`).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: `WS /api/v1/sessions/{name}/console` — attach an interactive PTY console to a CFS session pod via WebSocket.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Session name

parameters.name: cols
parameters.description: Initial terminal width in columns (default `80`).

parameters.name: rows
parameters.description: Initial terminal height in rows (default `24`).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/sessions/{name}/logs` — stream CFS session pod logs via Server-Sent Events.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Session name

parameters.name: timestamps
parameters.description: When true, prefix each log line with its timestamp.

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

get.summary: GET /templates — list BOS session templates with optional filters.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Exact template name.

parameters.name: hsm_group
parameters.description: HSM group whose associated templates should be returned.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Cap on the number of templates returned (most recent first).

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/templates/{name}/sessions` — create a BOS session from a session template.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Template name

parameters.name: X-Manta-Site
parameters.description: Name of the target cluster (matches a site configured in the server).

AddHwComponentRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/hardware-clusters/{target}/members`.

Moves nodes matching `pattern` out of `parent_cluster` and into
the path-level target cluster.

create_hsm_group.description: Create the target HSM group if it does not already exist.

dry_run.description:
When true, return the planned changes without modifying group
membership.

parent_cluster.description: Source HSM group that donates nodes matching `pattern`.

pattern.description: Hardware component pattern used to select which nodes to move.

dry_run.description: When true, return the computed changeset without applying it.

hsm_group.description: Target HSM group; all members are resolved to xnames.

overwrite.description: When true, overwrite parameters that already exist.

params.description: Space-separated kernel parameter `key=value` pairs to add.

xnames_expression.description:
Hosts expression (xnames, NIDs, or hostlist notation); mutually
exclusive with `hsm_group`.

enabled.description:
Whether to register the node as enabled. Defaults to `false`
(disabled) per serde's default for `bool`; the CLI's
`manta add node` flips the polarity via `--disabled`.

group.description: Initial HSM group the node belongs to.

id.description: Physical location ID (xname) of the node, e.g. `x3000c0s1b0n0`.

AddNodeResponse.description: Response for `POST /api/v1/nodes` — echoes the registered xname.

id.description: Physical location ID (xname) of the registered node.

hosts_expression.description:
Hostlist expression (xnames, NIDs, or hostlist notation)
identifying the new member set for the group.

AddNodesToGroupResponse.description:
Response body for `POST /api/v1/groups/{name}/members`.

The `removed` field name is retained for wire stability; its value
is the final, sorted membership of the group after the update —
**not** a list of removed nodes.

added.description: Xnames that were added to the group as part of this request.

final_members.description: Final, sorted membership of the group after the update.

removed.description:
Deprecated alias for [`Self::final_members`]. Carries the same
value so existing clients reading `removed` keep working for one
release; new clients should read `final_members`. Scheduled for
removal in the next major bump — at which point the
`#[serde(alias = "removed")]` on `final_members` keeps inbound
compatibility for anyone POSTing back the old name.

ApplyBootConfigRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/boot-config`.

Applies a combined boot configuration (image + runtime config +
kernel parameters) to the nodes named by `hosts_expression`. The
field is a hosts expression — xnames, NIDs, or hostlist notation;
HSM group names are not accepted here (resolve them client-side
first if needed).

boot_image_configuration.description:
CFS configuration name associated with the boot image; the
server resolves the most recent image built against this
configuration when `boot_image_id` is absent.

boot_image_id.description: IMS image ID to set as the boot image.

dry_run.description: When true, return the computed changeset without persisting it.

hosts_expression.description:
Hosts expression (xnames, NIDs, or hostlist notation) naming
the target nodes.

runtime_configuration.description: CFS configuration to assign as the runtime desired-config.

create_target_hsm_group.description: Create the target HSM group if absent (default `true`).

delete_empty_parent_hsm_group.description: Delete the parent HSM group if it becomes empty (default `true`).

dry_run.description:
When true, return the planned changes without modifying group
membership.

mode.description:
Whether to pin nodes into the target cluster or unpin them back
to the parent. Defaults to `Pin`.

pattern.description: Hardware component pattern selecting which nodes to pin/unpin.

dry_run.description: When true, return the computed changeset without applying it.

hsm_group.description: Target HSM group; all members are resolved to xnames.

operation.description: Which mutation to perform: add, apply (replace), or delete.

xnames_expression.description:
Hosts expression (xnames, NIDs, or hostlist notation); mutually
exclusive with `hsm_group`.

password.description:
Keycloak password. Never logged by the server (the
`/auth/*` sub-router is wrapped by `strip_body_for_logs`).

username.description: Keycloak username submitted to the configured backend.

token.description:
Bearer token issued by the backend (CSM or OpenCHAMI Keycloak).
Pass this as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every
subsequent request.

BackendSummary.description:
One row of the backend-data summary, anchored on an IMS image.

Every IMS image visible to the caller produces exactly one row.
The row's `image_id` and `name` are always populated; the rest are
`Option<String>` and are filled in only when the corresponding
relation resolves.

bos_sessiontemplate.description:
BOS session template whose `boot_sets[*].path` references this
`image_id`. If multiple templates match, the first one in name
order wins.

bos_sessiontemplate_boot_image.description:
Echo of `image_id` whenever `bos_sessiontemplate` is `Some(_)`.
Carried explicitly so the row is self-describing.

configuration_name.description:
CFS configuration the image was built with
(`Image.configuration`).

session_configuration_name.description:
That session's own `configuration.name`. Usually equals
`configuration_name` but they can drift if the image was
re-tagged after the session completed.

session_name.description:
CFS session whose `status.artifacts[*].result_id` contains
this `image_id`. If multiple sessions match, the first one in
name order wins.

session_result_id.description:
Echo of `image_id` whenever `session_name` is `Some(_)`. Carried
explicitly so the row is self-describing.

BosOperation.description: BOS session operation to run against the template's node list.

CompletedResponse.description:
Response for endpoints that simply confirm a backup / restore /
long-running migrate operation finished. Emitted by
`POST /api/v1/migrate/backup` and `POST /api/v1/migrate/restore`.

ConfigurationAnalysis.description: One row of the configuration-deletion-safety analysis.

safe_to_delete.description:
`true` if no CFS component lists this configuration as its
`desired_config` **and** no image built from this configuration
is referenced by any BSS boot-parameter record. A row that is
not safe is referenced by at least one of those two surfaces.

image_id.description: IMS image ID to boot the ephemeral environment from.

CreateImageCfsSessionRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/sat-file/images/cfs-session`.

Carries one entry from the SAT file's `images` section plus the
CLI's accumulated `ref_lookup` and the ansible knobs the CFS
session needs.

ansible_verbosity.description:
Ansible verbosity level (0–4) for the CFS session that builds
the image.

dry_run.description:
Validate without creating; the server returns a mocked complete
session with a `DRYRUN-<uuid>` result id.

image.description: One SAT `images[]` entry as a structured value.

ref_lookup.description:
`ref_name.or(name) -> image_id` map for previously-created
images. The backend uses it to resolve `base.image_ref` chains.

CreateSessionRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/sessions`.

The CLI submits this when the user runs `manta run session`; the
server deserialises it in `handlers::session::create_session`.
`repo_names` and `repo_last_commit_ids` are parallel-indexed —
`repo_last_commit_ids[i]` is the commit SHA for `repo_names[i]`.

ansible_limit.description:
Ansible `--limit` expression restricting which xnames are
targeted (the service-layer authz check rejects group names —
pre-resolve them client-side).

cfs_conf_sess_name.description:
Explicit name for the CFS session and configuration;
auto-generated when absent.

playbook_yaml_file_name.description: Ansible playbook filename inside the repository.

repo_last_commit_ids.description: Git commit SHAs matching each entry in `repo_names`.

CreateSessionResponse.description:
Response for `POST /api/v1/sessions` — names of the created CFS
session and its underlying configuration.

configuration_name.description: Name of the CFS configuration the session was attached to.

session_name.description: Name of the created CFS session.

CreatedResponse.description:
Response for endpoints that simply confirm a write happened.

Emitted by `POST /api/v1/redfish-endpoints`,
`POST /api/v1/boot-parameters`, and `POST /api/v1/groups`.

hosts.description: Xnames whose BSS boot-parameter entries should be deleted.

dry_run.description:
When true, validate the request without modifying group
membership.

xnames_expression.description:
Hosts expression (xnames, NIDs, or hostlist notation)
identifying nodes to remove.

delete_hsm_group.description:
Delete the target HSM group if it becomes empty after the
operation.

dry_run.description:
When true, return the planned changes without modifying group
membership.

parent_cluster.description:
Destination HSM group that receives nodes moved out of the
target cluster.

pattern.description:
Hardware component pattern used to select which nodes to move
back.

dry_run.description: When true, return the computed changeset without applying it.

hsm_group.description: Target HSM group; all members are resolved to xnames.

params.description:
Space-separated parameter names (or `key=value` pairs) to
remove.

xnames_expression.description:
Hosts expression (xnames, NIDs, or hostlist notation); mutually
exclusive with `hsm_group`.

EphemeralEnvResponse.description:
Response for `POST /api/v1/ephemeral-env` — the freshly provisioned
ephemeral host.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard JSON error body returned by all failed endpoints.

error.description:
Human-readable explanation of the failure. Never includes
stack traces, credentials, or internal type names.

HwClusterMode.description:
Whether the hw cluster operation moves nodes into the target (Pin) or
releases them back (Unpin).

KernelParamOp.description:
Which kernel-parameter mutation to perform on
`POST /api/v1/kernel-parameters/apply`.

bos.description: BOS session template name (or filter) to back up.

destination.description: Filesystem path where backup files will be written.

MigrateNodesPairResult.description:
One migration pair's result; mirrors
`manta_server::service::migrate::NodeMigrationResult` on the wire.
Embedded inside [`MigrateNodesResponse`] without importing the
server-side type into the shared crate.

parent_hsm_name.description: HSM group that the nodes were moved out of.

parent_members.description: Remaining member list of the parent group after migration.

target_hsm_name.description: HSM group that received the nodes.

target_members.description: Final member list of the target group after migration.

create_hsm_group.description: Create the target HSM group if it does not already exist.

dry_run.description:
When true, validate the migration plan without modifying any
group membership.

hosts_expression.description:
Node-set expression (xnames, NIDs, or hostlist notation)
selecting which nodes to migrate.

parent_hsm_names.description: Source HSM group names the nodes currently belong to.

target_hsm_names.description: Destination HSM group names to move nodes into.

MigrateNodesResponse.description:
Response for `POST /api/v1/migrate/nodes` — moved xnames plus a
per-(target,parent) result list. Dry-run uses the same shape so the
CLI consumes one type regardless of mode.

xnames.description: Xnames moved (or that would have been moved, in dry-run).

bos_file.description: Path to the BOS session template backup file.

cfs_file.description: Path to the CFS configuration backup file.

hsm_file.description: Path to the HSM group backup file.

image_dir.description: Directory containing the image layer tarballs.

ims_file.description: Path to the IMS image metadata backup file.

overwrite.description:
When true, overwrite existing resources that conflict with the
backup.

NodeDetails.description:
Per-node details returned by `GET /api/v1/nodes`.

Mirror of `csm_rs::node::types::NodeDetails` with identical fields
and identical JSON wire format. No conversion impl is needed in
the server crate — the response is serialised straight from the
csm-rs type and the CLI deserialises it into this mirror.

All fields are wire-stringified (CSM serializes them that way);
callers parse them as needed for display or comparison.

boot_configuration.description: CFS configuration linked to the boot image.

boot_image_id.description: IMS image ID currently set as the boot image.

desired_configuration.description: CFS desired-configuration name targeting this node.

enabled.description:
`"true"` or `"false"` — whether the node is enabled in the
hardware state manager.

error_count.description: Stringified count of recent CFS failures.

hsm.description: Comma-separated HSM group names this node belongs to.

kernel_params.description: Kernel command-line parameters as last reported by BSS.

nid.description: Numeric node ID as a string, e.g. `"nid001313"`.

power_status.description:
Current power state reported by PCS (`"On"`, `"Off"`, `"Ready"`,
etc.).

PostSatConfigurationRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/sat-file/configurations`.

Carries one entry from the SAT file's `configurations` section
plus per-call flags. csm-rs owns the SAT schema; the CLI and server
just shuttle the entry through as `serde_json::Value`.

configuration.description: One SAT `configurations[]` entry as a structured value.

dry_run.description:
Validate without creating; the response contains a mock
configuration.

overwrite.description: Overwrite an existing CFS configuration of the same name.

PostSatSessionTemplateRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/sat-file/session-templates`.

Carries one entry from the SAT file's `session_templates` section
plus the CLI's accumulated `ref_lookup` and per-call flags.

dry_run.description:
Validate without creating; the response contains a mock template
and, if `reboot` was set, no session is returned.

reboot.description:
After creating the template, trigger a BOS session to reboot the
targeted nodes through it.

ref_lookup.description:
`ref_name.or(name) -> image_id` map for previously-created
images; the backend uses it to resolve `image.image_ref`.

session_template.description: One SAT `session_templates[]` entry as a structured value.

PostSatSessionTemplateResponse.description:
Response body for `POST /api/v1/sat-file/session-templates`.

`session` is populated when `reboot` was true and a BOS session
was created.

session.description: The BOS session created by the reboot, if any.

template.description: The created (or mock, in dry-run) BOS session template.

dry_run.description:
When true, validate the session parameters without creating a
BOS session.

include_disabled.description: When true, include nodes marked as disabled.

limit.description:
Ansible limit expression restricting which template nodes are
targeted.

operation.description: BOS operation to run (boot, reboot, or shutdown).

session_name.description:
Optional explicit name for the BOS session; auto-generated when
absent.

PowerAction.description: The power operation to apply to a list of xnames.

force.description:
Pass `--force` to the underlying power operation (forceful
shutdown/reset).

host_expression.description:
For `Nodes`: hosts expression (xnames, NIDs, or hostlist
notation). For `Cluster`: the HSM group name.

target_type.description:
Whether `host_expression` is a node expression or a cluster
name.

PowerTargetType.description:
Whether the caller's `host_expression` is a hosts expression
(xnames / NIDs / hostlist) or a single HSM group name whose
members should be targeted.

cfs_session_name.description:
Name of the (already terminal-complete) CFS session whose result
image should be stamped with `manta.image_session.*` provenance.

UpdateBootParametersParams.description: Typed parameters for updating boot parameters.

initrd.description: S3 path to the initrd image.

kernel.description: S3 path to the kernel image.

macs.description: MAC addresses corresponding to `hosts` (optional alternate identifier).

nids.description: Node IDs corresponding to `hosts` (optional alternate identifier).

UpdateRedfishEndpointParams.description: Typed parameters for updating/adding a Redfish endpoint.

domain.description: Domain portion of the BMC FQDN.

enabled.description: Whether the endpoint is enabled for discovery.

fqdn.description: Full FQDN; overrides hostname+domain when set.

hostname.description: Hostname portion of the BMC FQDN.

mac_required.description: Whether a MAC address is required for geolocation.

rediscover_on_update.description: Trigger a rediscovery pass when the endpoint is updated.

template_id.description: ID of a discovery template to apply.

use_ssdp.description: Use SSDP for automatic endpoint discovery.

token.description: Bearer token to validate against the backend.
